results.title: Introducing Shot Lists & Storyboards: Customizable and Intuitive Interface
excerpts:
# Introducing Shot Lists: Customizable and Intuitive Interface
By [Shant Kiraz](https://www.studiobinder.com/author/shantkiraz/) [on August 9, 2024](https://www.studiobinder.com/blog/shot-lists-complete-customization-intuitive-interface/)
N ow you can plan out your shots and share them with your team more efficiently in StudioBinder. Easily upload images to your shot list, daisy chain shot details, and customize your workflow.
But we’re just scratching the surface.
Let’s dive into the details.
More...

## **1. Incredibly fast and powerful**
We’ve made it easy and fast to select shot details quickly. Just select from an **extensive** list of shot sizes, shot types, camera movements and equipment to plan your shots in no time.
Use hotkeys to add, edit or duplicate new shots super fast. Work scene-by-scene from your stripboard for a seamless experience.

## **2. Customize to make it your own**
Customize your layout to perfectly fit your needs. Filter columns, upload images, change aspect ratios, add notes, color-code each shot and a much more.  The following are all columns you can enable or disable on-the-fly:
* Scene #
* Shot #
* Subject
* Description
* Camera
* Shot Size
* Shot Type
* Movement
* Equipment
* Lens
* Special Equipment

## **3. Preview scenes with a click**
If you imported a script, we automatically generate shot lists for every scene. Just select a scene and start adding shots. You can just as easily preview the script for each scene so you don’t miss any important shots.

## **4. Minimize back-and forth and collaborate **_easier_****
It’s more efficient to keep one living shot list, instead of combing through folders and emails for PDF versions.
Invite teammates and your DP to collaborate in-app to create the shot list together. We’ve integrated [WorkFlow](https://www.studiobinder.com/film-production-management-software/) along the edge of your shot list so it’s easier than ever to assign tasks, upload images, and post to comments for your team.

...

## **Share your vision with elegant shot lists and storyboards.**
Create robust and customizable shot lists. Upload images to make storyboards and slideshows.
[Learn ﻿Mo﻿re﻿﻿ ➜](https://www.studiobinder.com/shot-list-storyboard/)
[**Get Started for Free**](https://app.studiobinder.com/register)
Tags: [Shot Listing](https://www.studiobinder.com/tag/shot-listing/)
* [Shant Kiraz](https://www.studiobinder.com/author/shantkiraz/)
  Managing Director @ StudioBinder. Find me online instagramming, facebooking, and tweeting. Also a photographer, nature enthusiast, and avid fruit muncher.

results.title: Shot Lister - App Store - Apple
excerpts:
# Shot Lister
Film shot list & schedule tool
Free · In‑App Purchases
Share
* 177 Ratings 4.6 1. 2. 3. 4. 5.
* Ages 4+ Years
* Category Photo & Video
* [Developer Reel Apps Inc.](https://apps.apple.com/us/developer/reel-apps-inc/id529436221?platform=iphone)
* Language EN English
* Size 51.5 MB
iPhone, iPad, Mac
Stop wasting time scribbling over paper shot lists when time matters most. Now you can make your day, the easy way. SHOT LISTER comes with all the features you will need to Shot List and build a comprehensive shot list for your shoot for free. Extra professional features require a Pro subscription. •LIVE MODE The only software available to easily create a shooting schedule on a shot by shot & minute by minute basis. Experience the truly ground breaking “Live mode” to see exactly how you are doing to the minute and re-organize the day in a matter of seconds. Shot Lister shows you exactly how many minutes ahead or behind you are, so you don’t have to do math while you shoot. •INDUSTRY GRADE Easily organize & store hundreds of scenes & thousands of shots with the flick of a finger. We support scene letter revisions, 1/8 page counts, “i” and “o” exclusion & cast breakdown tracking. All to fit a variety of professional workflows.

...

Keep the entire crew up to date live. Allow multiple units to be making changes simultaneous. Also manage crew privileges as “view only” or “view and edit” to share authorship with collaborators. •SCRIPT IMPORTING (PRO) Import Final Draft or Fountain scripts & let Shot Lister instantly build a project, generating all the scene meta data & cast list to save time. Convert Celtx to Fountain to import. •EPISODES (PRO) A must have for any television series. Import the script for your next episode into your existing project and schedule scenes from different episodes that share locations, cast or other commonalities on the same day. •STORYBOARDS (PRO) Draw or add Storyboards to every shot & display full screen in Storyboard Theatre. View your plan, see which boards are left to be shot in Shooting order. •CIRCLE TAKES (PRO) In the time it takes to tap a finger, circle the best takes & export the data as PDF.

# Shot List Software
## Call the Shots
### StudioBinder’s shot list maker is designed to fit any scale of production
[Get Started for Free](https://app.studiobinder.com/register?documents)
▶ Watch Tour

...

## A shot list creator designed for professionals
Tag shots directly from the script, populate your shot specs, customize the layout, build your shooting schedule, and collaborate with your entire team. Take your shot listing game to the next level.

...

## Choose your layout
Keep things as simple or detailed as needed by filtering the columns to show what you want and hide what you don’t.
This is your project, make it your own.

...

## **A creative process for the modern era**
Creating a shot list has always been a collaboration between the director, cinematographer, and 1st AD. StudioBinder brings that process online, and makes it easy, efficient, and shareable.
**Director**
The director builds the core shot list to express the creative vision.
**Director of Photography**
The DP fills in technical details like equipment, lens, lighting setups, etc.
**1st Assistant Director**
The 1st AD works in the necessary time estimates to schedule the day.

## **Status?
Check ✓**
Nothing builds momentum on set more than checking off completed shots. Track your day’s progress on your phone. See what’s done, and what’s left to do.
Best of all — anyone you share the shot list with can can follow along on their own phones.

results.title: How to Make a Lined Script and Save Your Shot List - StudioBinder
excerpts:
# How to Make a Lined Script and Save Your Shot List
## HOW TO MAKE A LINED SCRIPT AND SAVE YOUR SHOT LIST
### 2. How to create a lined script
[Filling out your shot list template](https://www.studiobinder.com/blog/how-to-make-a-shot-list/) , then taking your [shot list](https://www.studiobinder.com/blog/what-is-a-shot-list-example/) and lining the portions of a scene each shot covers begins the process of translating what you see in your head into the tangible medium of film and video.
You want potential issues to come to light now so you can plan for them. This is especially true on an independent [film production](https://www.studiobinder.com/blog/what-is-film-production-definition/) where you'll be wearing multiple hats ( [managing the parking situation](https://www.studiobinder.com/blog/crew-parking-filming-on-location/) , for example.)
Wearing multiple hats equals an increased chance of missing something.
But with a little preparation and a lined script, you can set up your camera positions and get everything you need.

...

## **Share your vision with elegant shot lists and storyboards.**
Create robust and customizable shot lists. Upload images to make storyboards and slideshows.
[Learn ﻿Mo﻿re﻿﻿ ➜](https://www.studiobinder.com/shot-list-storyboard/)
[**Get Started for Free**](https://app.studiobinder.com/register)
Tags: [Camera Shots](https://www.studiobinder.com/tag/camera-shots/) , [Shot List App](https://www.studiobinder.com/tag/shot-list-app/) , [Shot Listing](https://www.studiobinder.com/tag/shot-listing/)
* [Frank Dellario](https://www.studiobinder.com/author/frank-dellario/)
  Frank produces video, animation, and other media at Pixel Valley Studio in the SF Bay area. He was at the forefront of the machinima animation movement since its beginnings in 1997, when he co-founded the award-winning team the ILL Clan in Brooklyn, NY. He was also the publisher and co-founder of the trade FilmCrew magazine. He has produced, directed and filmed multiple productions for clients such as CBS, Paramount, Universal, NBC, Warner Brothers, Intel, MTV, SpikeTV, and Microsoft. He currently produces and co-stars in the video production how-to series Pull My Focus.
